evidence_quotes.quote: some video games can be deleted or neutralised by their publisher when they wish to do so.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I join hundreds of others who think that the Digital Fairness Act should also apply to the protection of video games that are threatened with removal after purchase. At present, some video games can be deleted or neutralised by their publisher when they wish to do so. Buying a product that can at any time be neutralised remotely and without justification by the publisher of that product seems to be a profound injustice for consumer rights. Imagine that a DVD or a tablet or drill electronic tool could be deactivated at any time as desired by the structure from which you bought it. This is unthinkable. The European ciotyan initiative Stop Killing Games, which is close to the 1.5 million signatures, is precisely about changing legislation on these issues. I hope you will understand my position and take it into account. Thank you
